# Combat Guide

Melee combat is a double layer of rock-paper-scissors: a stamina-and-poise duel
on top of a damage-type-versus-armor matchup. Nothing here touches the gathering
or crafting economy; it is a wholly separate system so retrieval has a clean
off-topic neighbour to distinguish against.

## Stamina And Poise

Every attack, dodge, and block spends stamina, and a fighter whose stamina pool
empties is briefly unable to act. Poise is the separate resistance that decides
whether an incoming hit staggers you; heavy attacks drain more poise than light
ones, and once poise breaks the victim is locked in a stagger animation and open
to a punish.

## Parry And Guard Break

A parry is a precisely timed block: the parry window opens for a few frames at the
start of the block, and a hit deflected inside that window opens the attacker to a
critical riposte. A guard break is the counter to a passive turtle — a charged
attack that shatters a held block, stripping the guard and staggering the blocker
regardless of how much stamina they were holding in reserve.

## Dodge And Invincibility Frames

The dodge roll grants invincibility frames — the i-frames — during which the
character passes harmlessly through an attack. The window is generous at the start
of the roll and closes before the recovery, so a well-timed roll beats an
unblockable grab that a block cannot stop, but a panicked early roll recovers
straight into the follow-up hit.

## Damage Types

Attacks deal one of three damage types: slash, pierce, and crush. Slash excels
against unarmored flesh, pierce is built to punch through plate and defeat rigid
protection, and crush ignores flexible padding to transmit force straight through
to the body underneath. Choosing the damage type your target is weak to matters
more than raw attack power.

## Armor Tiers

Armor comes in three tiers that each answer a different damage type. Light armor
is flexible cloth and leather that shrugs off crush but parts under a slash; medium
armor is mail that turns a slash yet is defeated by a pierce; heavy armor is rigid
plate that stops slash and pierce cold but transmits crush straight through to the
wearer. Read the armor, pick the damage type, win the exchange.
